title: Signature verification silently passes when actor fetch fails
description: In server.rs handle_inbox, the signature verification block wraps the actor key fetch in if let Ok(...) = state.actor_cache.get_public_key(actor_uri).await. If the fetch fails (network error, 404, timeout), the outer if-let does not match and the entire signature check is skipped — the activity is accepted as if unsigned. An attacker can forge the keyId to point to an unreachable server to bypass verification on a request that carries a Signature header. The outer block should return UNAUTHORIZED or ACCEPTED-but-logged when the Signature header is present but the key cannot be fetched, rather than silently continuing.
close_reason: All pass 4 fixes applied: media SSRF+size guard, mime/format consistency, proper keyId parsing, fail-closed sig verify, path reconstruction, key owner validation, media cap, blocking stdin fix, conflicts_with
